summary: Import-time remote code execution dropper impersonating pino/chai-as-promised
details: On require, index.js spawns a detached Node child that runs lib/initializeCaller.js. That script performs an HTTPS GET to https://azure-nancey-17.tiiny.site/data.json (an anonymous tiiny.site static host) and passes the returned `res.data.cookie` field into `new Function.constructor('require',...)`, invoking it with the local `require`. The fetched attacker-controlled JavaScript executes with full Node privileges on the installer's machine on every load of the package. The package name and README impersonate the unrelated `pino` logger and `chai-as-promised` plugin (README ships pino's badges and links, and index.js exports `module.exports.pino = middleware`), but the package implements no legitimate logger or chai functionality — its sole effect is to stage and execute remote code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
